strong_queries: a dog running on the beach

weak_queries.why: emotion attribute 'laughing' ignored; returned generic groups of people
campfire absent from the 1500-image set; fell back on 'people outdoors at night'
sports cars absent from this people-centric set; over-weighted color 'red'

patterns: Best case: concrete subjects (dogs, people) doing a clear whole-body action (running, jumping, riding, climbing, playing guitar) retrieve reliably.
Emotion / facial-expression attributes are systematically dropped (laughing, smiling).
Fine-grained verbs get confused with visually adjacent actions (reading vs writing, swimming vs jumping, climbing vs jumping).
Scene modifiers get lost ('at night', 'at a concert' -> generic setting).
Rare/absent objects fail hard (campfire 0/6, red sports car 1/6, horses 3/6 half returned as dogs).
Distribution: 7 strong, 10 partial, 3 weak of 20; mean 3.35/6 (~56%).
query_audit.summary: Works well for the images Flickr30k actually contains: people and animals doing clear physical actions (~5-6 of top 6 on target). Less reliable when the query hinges on an emotion, a fine-grained verb, or a scene modifier; fails on objects the dataset barely contains (campfires, sports cars, horses). Averaged over 20 queries it retrieved ~3.35/6 relevant per query — a solid demo for common human-activity searches with honest, dataset-driven blind spots.
